title: Bridge Essay: Somatosensory coding of visual self-identity.
concept: Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership as constructed percept.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership as constructed percept.

**Somatosensory coding of visual self-identity.** - Neuroimage (2025). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121405. PMID: 40744249. How does the brain process our bodily identity? This question has long fascinated scientists because of its potential implications for the study of self-awareness. Here, to test the idea that the somatosensory system is directly involved in coding bodily self-identity even when conveyed through vision, we probed the somatosensory system with tactile stimuli while participants observed hand images, either belonging to them (self-hand) or to another person (other-hand).
